intro: Caputia medley-woodii, also known as medley-wood's caputia or yellow caputia, is a plant species of the genus Caputia, a group of flowering plants in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Caputia medley-woodii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared for their purported therapeutic effects.
